claimText: The Solar Energy Corporation of India (SECI) was established in 2011 specifically to implement the National Solar Mission.
evidenceExcerpt: Established in 2011 for the implementation of the National Solar Mission
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and primary mandate of SECI.
distractorHooks: Established for the National Green Hydrogen Mission
Established in 2015 after the Paris Agreement
Established under the Ministry of Power

claimText: SECI is a Navratna Central Public Sector Undertaking (CPSU) operating under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
evidenceExcerpt: SECI is a Navratna Central Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).
examUse: Statement-based question testing the organizational status and nodal ministry of SECI.
distractorHooks: Under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

claimText: SECI functions as a Category-I power trading licensee under the Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (CERC).
evidenceExcerpt: functions as a Category-I power trading licensee under CERC.
examUse: Statement-based question on SECI's trading capabilities and regulatory oversight.
distractorHooks: Regulated by State Electricity Regulatory Commissions (SERCs)

claimText: SECI is responsible for implementing special regional renewable energy models, including the greening of islands like Lakshadweep and Andaman & Nicobar, and high-altitude grid-linked capacity in Ladakh.
evidenceExcerpt: SECI implements special programmes like the greening of islands (Lakshadweep, A&N Islands) and high-altitude grid-linked capacity in Ladakh.
examUse: Application or statement-based question on SECI's geographical scope and special projects.
distractorHooks: Implemented directly by the respective Union Territory administrations without SECI involvement
SECI's mandate is strictly limited to mainland solar parks
